case_name: Quinnell Reddick v. the State of Louisiana, D/B/A the Interim Lsu Public Hospital, Alan Bland Marr, M.D., Chiranjiv S. Virk, M.D., John P. Hunt, III, M.D. and Amir Abdul-Jabbar, M.D.

summary.brief: Quinnell Reddick v. the State of Louisiana, D/B/A the Interim Lsu Public Hospital, Alan Bland Marr, M.D., Chiranjiv S. Virk, M.D., John P. Hunt, III, M.D. and Amir Abdul-Jabbar, M.D.. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: In 2012, Ms. Reddick underwent a cervical MRI which revealed moderate sized nodules on the right lobe of her thyroid.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On September 18, 2012, under the supervision of Dr. Marr, Ms. Reddick underwent a right hemi-thyroidectomy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On April 29, 2013, Ms. Reddick was seen by Dr. Marr, Dr. Chiranjiv S. Virk and Dr. Amir Abdul-Jabbar as part of her right thyroid post-operative follow-up.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It was recommended that her left thyroid be removed because the right thyroid post-operative report revealed cancerous lesions on the right thyroid.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On this same date, Ms. Reddick signed the 'Patient Consent to Medical Treatment or Surgical Procedure and Acknowledgement of Receipt of Medical Information' for the left thyroidectomy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The post-operative report of the left thyroid did not reveal any evidence of malignancy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court found that Ms. Reddick failed to present any expert medical evidence establishing the requisite standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The trial court dismissed Ms. Reddick’s claims that defendants erroneously advised her that she had cancer and thus violated the appropriate standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We find there exists a genuine issue of material fact as to whether ultrasound observation was a reasonable therapeutic alternative to the surgical removal of Ms. Reddick’s left thyroid.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: We affirm the portion of the trial court’s judgment denying Ms. Reddick’s motion for partial summary judgment; reverse the portion of the judgment granting defendants’ cross motion for summary judgment; and remand the matter for further proceedings.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion
